content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of input and an intentional cognitive act of reviewing learned knowledge, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this dual framing to add a 'language learning-style' refinement step: after generating an initial output, LLMs should treat their own output as new 'learning input' to parse for implicit errors, rather than only checking against pre-defined reasoning perspectives. This bridges the linguistic educational definition of reflexion with technical LLM self-reflection architecture to unlock a simpler, more generalizable self-improvement pipeline.
